claimText: The IUCN World Heritage Outlook 4 Report categorizes the Western Ghats, Manas National Park, and Sundarbans National Park as being of 'significant concern'.
evidenceExcerpt: It categorises Western Ghats and 2 national parks (NP) in India – Assam’s Manas NP and West Bengal’s Sundarbans NP as being of “significant concern”.
examUse: Statement-based question on the conservation status of Indian sites in the IUCN World Heritage Outlook Report.
distractorHooks: Swapping the names of the national parks (e.g., Kaziranga instead of Manas)
Changing the status category to 'Critical' or 'Good'

claimText: Khangchendzonga National Park is the only Indian site that remained 'Good' in the Conservation Outlook Rating of the IUCN World Heritage Outlook 4 Report.
evidenceExcerpt: Only Khangchendzonga National Park remained ‘Good’ in Conservation Outlook Rating.
examUse: Fact-based statement identifying the only 'Good' rated Indian site in the report.
distractorHooks: Naming another prominent national park (e.g., Jim Corbett, Keoladeo) as the only 'Good' site
Stating that no Indian site achieved a 'Good' rating

claimText: The conservation outlook status of Sundarbans National Park deteriorated from 'Good with some Concerns' to 'Significant Concerns' between 2020 and 2025.
evidenceExcerpt: Sundarbans National Park: Witnessed Deterioration in its conservation outlook status from ‘Good with some Concerns’ to ‘Significant Concerns’ from 2020-2025.
examUse: Statement-based question tracking the changing conservation status of major Indian ecological sites.
distractorHooks: Stating the status improved rather than deteriorated
Attributing this specific deterioration to a different national park like Manas
